title: Download file handler does not check dropbox permissions using normalized/resolved paths
description: The receiveDownloadFile handler checks dropbox permissions using 'FilePrivilege(path: App.filesController.real(path: path))' where 'path' is the raw un-normalized user input. It does not use dropBoxPrivileges(forVirtualPath:) like other handlers do. This means: (1) it checks permissions on the raw concatenated path rather than the normalized virtual path, which could miss the dropbox ancestor detection, and (2) a path that would be blocked after normalization might pass the permission check before normalization. Furthermore, the permission check uses FilePrivilege(path:) directly on the file path rather than walking up to find the dropbox ancestor directory, so permissions are only checked if the target itself has a .wired/permissions file.
recommendation: Normalize the path first (like upload does), then use App.filesController.dropBoxPrivileges(forVirtualPath: normalizedPath) to correctly detect inherited dropbox permissions from ancestor directories.
